Operation 'ids.projects.locations.endpoints.create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description lacks a summary entirely and provides minimal context. Enhanced version clarifies: (1) what an Endpoint is in the Cloud IDS domain (forwarding rule for inspection), (2) required inputs (parent, location), (3) what is returned (Endpoint object with resource name), (4) side effects (provisioning initiated, asynchronous operation). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation, what parameters are needed, and how to handle the response.

data.current_description: Creates a new Endpoint in a given project and location.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new IDS inspection endpoint
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Endpoint resource in the specified project and location. An Endpoint defines a forwarding rule to which packets can be sent for IDS inspection. Requires the parent project and location identifiers. Returns the newly created Endpoint object with its assigned resource name and configuration. This operation initiates endpoint provisioning and may take time to complete; monitor the returned operation status for completion.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks a summary entirely and provides minimal context. Enhanced version clarifies: (1) what an Endpoint is in the Cloud IDS domain (forwarding rule for inspection), (2) required inputs (parent, location), (3) what is returned (Endpoint object with resource name), (4) side effects (provisioning initiated, asynchronous operation). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation, what parameters are needed, and how to handle the response.

message: Operation 'ids.projects.locations.operations.cancel' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details. Missing summary. Description is verbose but lacks clarity about input requirements (CancelOperationRequest) and return value (empty response). Reorganized for clarity while preserving important details about best-effort semantics and state transitions.

data.current_description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of `1`, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates asynchronous cancellation of a long-running operation. Accepts a CancelOperationRequest specifying the operation to cancel. The server makes a best effort to cancel but success is not guaranteed. Returns an empty response. On successful cancellation, the operation transitions to a CANCELLED state with an error status code of 1. Use Operations.GetOperation to verify cancellation status. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is verbose but lacks clarity about input requirements (CancelOperationRequest) and return value (empty response). Reorganized for clarity while preserving important details about best-effort semantics and state transitions.
